ref: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
text: Ta die Sitaani die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a baarɩ, “Dɩɩ yi fʋ seŋ yi Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa, vaa tanaha gie bɩrɩŋ nyindiike.”
translated: Then Satan said to the woman, "Did God really say you must not eat from any tree in the garden?"

ref: Finally, the devil took Jesus up on a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms on earth and their power.
text: Womi Sitaani wa die dɩ bɩ a chii Yisa a yallɩ wa a ga zieŋ taŋkpeŋkpɩɩŋ sikpeŋ a dagɩ Yisa dʋnɩa tɩgɩsɩ mana mana aŋaŋ ka nyinti mana ta balɩ a yɩ wa dɩ,
translated: Then Satan took him and showed him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment, and said to him, "I will give you all their authority and splendor, for it has been given to me, and I can give it to anyone I want to."

ref: News about him spread all over Syria, and people with every kind of sickness or disease were brought to him. Some of them had a lot of demons in them, others were thought to be crazy, and still others could not walk. But Jesus healed them all.
text: Die ba mʋʋlɩ ʋ saaŋ Siria tɩŋgbaŋ mana mana ma ma. Naa chɩaŋ ma vuosi die dɩ chii vuodiekemba die dɩ yalla yʋagɩtɩ mana mana a keŋ ʋ jigiŋ; jɩmbɩatɩ die dɩ yalla bataŋ, bataŋ die dɩ yallɩ kpeŋkpeŋsi aŋaŋ vuodiekemba nyɩŋgbanɩŋ die dɩ kpine ta die ʋ gbaaŋ ba mana.
translated: They went to the market to buy many different things. The chief sent his son to go and tell the people to come together; some of the people came quickly, others came slowly because they were far away and it took time for them to arrive.

ref: God blesses those people who are merciful. They will be treated with mercy!
text: Vuodiekemba dɩ chɩgɩnana ba chanchaalɩŋ zɔɔlɩŋ, sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ yiwo ba sɩɩtɩ, dama banɩŋ ba Ŋmɩŋ dɩaŋ dɩ nan chɩgɩ zɔɔlɩŋ.
translated: When the children are playing in the sand, their mothers sit and talk, while their fathers go to the farm to work in the sand.

ref: You are the light for the whole world. A city built on top of a hill cannot be hidden,
text: “Nɩnɩŋ nɩ sɩɩ sɩba tɩŋgbaŋka gie popoli; tɩŋ dieke ba mɩna a zieŋ kunkogiŋ sikpeŋ ka bɩagɩ a lɔbɩra.
translated: We went to the big market yesterday; there were many people buying and selling different things.

ref: and no one lights a lamp and puts it under a clay pot. Instead, it is placed on a lampstand, where it can give light to everyone in the house.
text: Ta vuoŋ ka chɔgɩsa popoli ta naga kpalɩ a bubike ama ʋ ŋaaŋ nagɩ ka a saagɩ wa jaaŋ ma jigidieke ka baaŋ nan chaaŋ a yɩ vuodiekemba mana dɩ benne tigiri me
translated: If you want to help people, you must first understand their problems and listen to them so that you can find a good way to support them and make things better for everyone.

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Do not murder” and “A murderer must be brought to trial.”
text: “Nɩ wʋŋya die ba balla kʋrɩŋ ma dɩ, ‘Da a keŋ a kʋʋ vuoŋ, fʋ a keŋ a kʋʋ vuoŋ ba nan chii fʋ ga naalɩaŋ a dii fʋ sarɩya.’
translated: When your mother tells you, ‘If you do this thing, or if you do that thing, you will see what I will do to you.’

ref: When you make a promise, say only “Yes” or “No.” Anything else comes from the devil. Revenge (Luke
text: Nɩ ko vaa aŋ nɩ ‘Wa’, yi nɩ ‘Wa’, ta nɩ ‘Aayɩ’ yi nɩ ‘Aayɩ’ dama nɩ hʋʋ jaaŋ a gʋtɩ, die nyɩŋ wa Sitaani jigiŋ. Da keŋ yiŋŋi a yi fʋ chanchaaŋ bɩaŋ (Luki
translated: If you say ‘Yes’, say ‘Yes’, and if you say ‘No’, say ‘No’, so that you will not fall into sin, like Satan did. This is what you should do (Luke)

ref: But I tell you not to try to get even with a person who has done something to you. When someone slaps your right cheek, turn and let that person slap your other cheek.
text: Ama manɩŋ lele n balɩ nɩ, nɩ da keŋ tuŋ nɩ chanchaaŋ hamɩŋ; vuoŋ dɩ keŋ a nɩgɩ fʋ nuudiigiŋ kaamɩŋ ma fʋ yiŋŋi fʋ nuugalɩ kaamɩŋ dɩaŋ a yɩ wa aŋ ʋ nɩgɩ;
translated: If you hear my words today, do not harden your heart; rather, open your mind to receive what I am telling you so that you may benefit from what I am saying to you.

ref: If you greet only your friends, what's so great about this? Don't even unbelievers do that?
text: Ta fʋ a keŋ waasa fʋ zʋalɩŋ nyɩɩna ma bie wʋvɩɩna fʋ yie a tɩaŋ fʋ chanchaalɩba? Daa die vuodiekemba dɩ kana a ka dɩ Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha dɩ yie?
translated: Did you go to the farm today or did you stay at home with your children? Or did something happen that made you not go?

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: “Fʋ keŋ yaala fʋ jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ, da a keŋ a yi sɩba gɩgaantɩba dɩ yinene die wo, dama banɩŋ ba cho dɩ ba zie Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juone me aŋaŋ siekpara ma tɩka sʋŋ a jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ dɩ vuosi ye be. Wusie maŋ bala nɩ ba wɔŋ ye ba tune mana mɩŋ.
translated: If you believe in God, then you will see the greatness that He has done for you, but if you do not believe and do not follow God’s way, you will not receive the blessings that He gives. Everyone should try to do what is right and follow His path.

ref: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
text: Ama fʋ keŋ a baa fʋ jʋʋsɩ Ŋmɩŋ, juu fʋ juoŋ me ta ligi sanʋarɩ, ta jʋʋsɩ fʋ chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ vuodieke dɩ benne lɔbɩrɩŋ ma, aŋ fʋ Chɔɔŋ wa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yene wudieke fʋ yine lɔbɩrɩŋ ma wa tuŋ fʋ )
translated: If you say you love God, but you hate your fellow human being, then you do not truly love God, because if you cannot love the person you see, how can you love God whom you do not see?

ref: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
text: “Fʋ a keŋ jʋʋsa Ŋmɩŋ da keŋ jʋʋsɩma aŋaŋ wʋbalɩkɩ wagɩtɩ sɩba vuodiekemba dɩ jɩannana bugile; banɩŋ ba yile sɩba ba keŋ jʋʋsɩ aŋaŋ wʋbalɩkɩ wagɩtɩ ba bugilehe nan wʋŋ ba.
translated: If you do good things, God will do good things for you and bless you with many good things; but if people do bad things, God will punish them for what they have done.

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
text: “Nɩ keŋ nagɩ nɩ chanchaalɩŋ wʋbɩatɩ a chaa ba, nɩ Chɔɔŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma dɩaŋ nan nagɩ nɩ tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa nɩ.
translated: If you take the small calabash and put it down, then God will take the big calabash and put it down too.

ref: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
text: Ama nɩ woliŋ yaalɩma Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ aŋaŋ wudieke Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yaalala wɩa ta ʋ nan nagɩ nyindiekemba nɩ yaalɩnana mana a yɩ nɩ.
translated: If you do not listen carefully to what I am telling you, you will not understand what I want you to do and you will make mistakes.

ref: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
text: Die wɩa da keŋ vaa soriŋ wɩa mugisi nɩ dama soriŋ nan dɩ yallɩ ka gbaŋ gbaŋ mugisikiŋ. Daaŋ mana yaa ka wumugisi dieke dɩ mʋgɩsɩna ka mɩŋ.
translated: If someone does not understand something, they should ask so that they can understand it well. There is nothing wrong with asking questions so that you can learn.

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Nɩ daansɩ a ye nembisi dɩ yʋgɩnana; a ka bʋra; a ka bɩ gobe ta nagɩ yie napooti me, ama nɩ Chɔɔŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma wa daansɩnana ha. Nɩ ka yaa nyʋarɩ a tɩaŋ nembisi?
translated: You said you would buy the medicine; you did not buy it; you did not even tell me the reason, but you know that the child is suffering a lot because of not buying it. What should we do about the medicine?

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: Ama n balɩ nɩ, halɩ naaŋ Solomoni aŋaŋ ʋ nyiŋyeeki vɩɩna mana die ka yeegi jayeekiŋ ka vɩɩna a tʋgɩ tɩɩwutehe gie.
translated: But as for me, I say, just as Solomon asked for wisdom to judge rightly and to lead the people well.

ref: Don't condemn others, and God won't condemn you.
text: “Nɩ da keŋ a dii nɩ chanchaalɩŋ sarɩya, amʋ Ŋmɩŋ dɩaŋ da keŋ dii nɩ sarɩya,
translated: If you say you love your neighbor, but God knows you don’t love your neighbor,

ref: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
text: “Yime fʋ chanchaalɩŋ wudieke fʋ baaŋ nan dɩ yaala ba yime a yɩma fʋ; dama Ŋmɩŋ mɩraha aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba dagɩkʋ dɩ dagɩna die wonde. Sanʋabiŋ juule wɩa (Luki
translated: If you forgive others their wrongs against you, your Father in heaven will forgive you also; but if you do not forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. (Luke)

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
text: Nɩɩŋ die dɩ paalɩ a nɩɩ, mʋga dɩ kpaa, ta bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ die dɩ faasɩ a nɩgɩ tigiri, ama tigiri die ka nanya, dama die ka zene taŋ ma wa chɩaŋ wɩa.
translated: If you take a stick and hit a dog, the dog will bark, but if you take a stone and hit a snake, the snake will not forget, because the snake waits for the right time to strike back.

ref: When Jesus finished speaking, the crowds were surprised at his teaching.
text: Die Yisa die dɩ balɩna wɩaha gie a kpatɩ, die dɩ yi daadamba mamachi aŋaŋ ʋ dagɩkʋ,
translated: When Yisa was going to the farm, he met Daadamba carrying firewood.

ref: Suddenly a man with leprosy came and knelt in front of Jesus. He said, “Lord, you have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
text: Womi gamɩŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ keŋ a gbigiri ʋ nɩŋŋa a baarɩ, “N Yɔmʋtieŋ, fʋ yaala nan bɩagɩ a gbaaŋ mɩŋ.”
translated: When evening came and they were sitting in the house, he said to his wife, "My dear, please bring me some water to drink."

ref: He took her by the hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served Jesus a meal.
text: Die Yisa die dɩ gbɩ ʋ nuuŋ; ta nyɩŋgbaŋ tuulibu die dɩ kpatɩ, die ʋ hagɩ a yi ba chaantɩ.
translated: If you see that something is good, and you do not say it, you will not get any benefit from it.

ref: Another disciple said to Jesus, “Lord, let me wait till I bury my father.”
text: Tɔ, ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba wʋnyɩ dɩaŋ die dɩ keŋ baarɩ, “N Yɔmʋtieŋ, vaa n ye woliŋ kuli a ga a guu n chʋa.”
translated: So, he called his children and said to them, "My children, come and let us eat together so that we may be happy."

ref: After Jesus left in a boat with his disciples,
text: Yisa die dɩ ga a juu haarɩŋ wʋnɩŋ aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ ga.
translated: Take this and go and give it to your father before he leaves.

ref: The men in the boat were amazed and said, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him.” Two Men with Demons in Them (Mark
text: Die dɩ yi be mamachi, ba pɩasa taŋ dɩ, “Daa lalɩa wʋnna? Halɩ bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ aŋaŋ nyaaŋ poŋŋiŋ dɩ wʋmma yɩa wa.” Yisa die dɩ gbaanna dembisi bale die jɩmbɩatɩ die dɩ yalla ba dene (Maki
translated: When they got to the woman, they asked her, “Where are you going? Why are you carrying that big load on your head?” The woman stopped and looked at them before she answered (Maki

ref: So the demons begged Jesus, “If you force us out, please send us into those pigs!”
text: Jɩmbɩatɩtɩ die dɩ jʋʋsɩ Yisa a baarɩ, “Fʋ keŋ yagɩ tɩ, fʋ vaa tɩ ga juu perukusisi me.”
translated: Jesus said to him, "If you are able, you can make me clean."

ref: Jesus told them to go, and they went out of the men and into the pigs. All at once the pigs rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
text: Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ha, “Nɩ gamma.” Die wɩa die a nyɩŋ dembisi bale wo me, a ga a juu perukusisi sʋŋ; Die perukusisi mana dɩ chɩgɩ birrr sʋʋŋ tanɩ chɩaŋ a ga nan mʋgɩkpɩɩrɩ ma ta nyaabʋ dɩ dii he.
translated: He took the thing and said, “Let’s go.” When he got there, he put the medicine down; The medicine was to be used for treating someone who was very sick, so that the person would recover and not die.

ref: But I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man, “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
text: N nan wa a dagɩ aŋ nɩ sɩmma a baarɩ manɩŋ vuota Bʋa yaa yiko dʋnɩa ma ma a nan bɩagɩ a nagɩ vuosi tʋmbɩatɩ a chaa ba”. Die wɩa die ʋ balɩ a yɩ yʋagɩtieŋ wo, “Hagɩ a nagɩ fʋ kalɩŋŋa a kulime.”
translated: “I said to him that if he does not listen to his father, God will not bless him in this world or the next. He should respect his father and obey his words.” Then they replied, “Let him obey his father and follow his advice.”

ref: They were able to see, and Jesus strictly warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: Die ba ninehe die dɩ yuori. Yisa die dɩ kpaaŋ ba aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna dɩ, “Nɩ da keŋ a balɩ a yɩ vuoŋ.”
translated: They have not seen them yet. Then they called one of them and said, "Go and see where they are."

ref: Philip, Bartholomew, Thomas, Matthew the tax collector, James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles.
text: aŋaŋ Filipi aŋaŋ Batolomiwo; aŋaŋ Tomasi aŋaŋ Matiwo, vuodieke die dɩ yine lampotuosiru wo; aŋaŋ Jemisi vuodieke dɩ yine Alifusi bʋa aŋaŋ Tadeusi,
translated: Philip and Bartholomew; Thomas and Matthew, the tax collector; James son of Alphaeus and Thaddeus,

ref: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
text: Dama wudiekemba nɩ bala a baa nɩ balɩ wa kaaŋ nyɩŋ nɩ jigiŋ, ama nɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩbʋ nan dɩ bie nɩ sʋŋ a bala.
translated: The chief sat down with the elders and they discussed the matter, but the Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ Haalɩbʋ said he would not agree to it.

ref: When people mistreat you in one town, hurry to another one. I promise you before you have gone to all the towns of Israel, the Son of Man will come.
text: Ta ba keŋ mugisi nɩ tɩŋ kaanɩ ma, nɩ chɩgɩ a ga tɩgɩgaaŋ. Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, nɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ kpatɩ nɩ tʋʋmaha Izara tɩgɩsɩ mana ma ta manɩŋ Vuota Bʋa keŋ.
translated: If you do not listen to what I am saying, you will not understand me. When someone speaks, you should pay attention so that you will know what the person is telling you, just as Izara told me yesterday at Vuota Bʋa.

ref: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
text: “Bʋbʋgɩrʋ ka tɩaŋ ʋ dɩdagɩrʋ; yɔmʋ dɩaŋ ka tɩaŋ ʋ tieŋ.
translated: The old woman is sitting in the house; the child is also sitting outside.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
text: Ba daa nembisi ale wo kobu, ama dɩ ka yi tɩ Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ dʋŋŋʋ, a kaanɩ mana kaaŋ nan tɩŋgbaŋ.
translated: If you do not listen to what I am saying, when you go to the house of God, you will not know what to do there.

ref: If you tell others you belong to me, I will tell my Father in heaven you are my followers.
text: “Vuodieke mana dɩ a keŋ a vʋarɩ ʋ gbaŋ vuosi me dɩ ʋ yiwo n vuoŋ, die gbaŋ gbaŋ maŋ nan vʋarɩ wa yaalɩŋ ma n Chɔɔŋ Ŋmɩŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma jigiŋ.
translated: When you go to the farm and you see a snake, do not kill it because the snake is a messenger of the ancestors and it brings blessings.

ref: But if you reject me, I will tell my Father in heaven you don't belong to me. Not Peace, but Trouble (Luke
text: Ama vuodieke dɩ baarɩ ʋ ka yi n vuoŋ vuosi jigiŋ, die gbaŋ gbaŋ maŋ nan balɩ dɩ n ka sɩba wa n Chɔɔŋ dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma jigiŋ. Puole wɩa (Luki
translated: The woman went to the market and bought some yams, then she returned home and cooked them for her children. After that, she (Luki)

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: “Nɩ da keŋ a yilime sɩba sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ maŋ yaa keŋ tɩŋgbaŋka gie vuosi jigiŋ; Aayɩ, n ka keŋ aŋaŋ sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ, ama n keŋ wo aŋaŋ puole.
translated: If you say you want to help me but when it is time for work you run away; well, I call that help, but I call you a liar.

ref: If you love your father or mother or even your sons and daughters more than me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: “Vuodieke mana dɩ chone ʋ chɔɔŋ yaa ʋ nuŋ a tɩaŋ mɩŋ ka mʋ ʋ yi n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ; vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ chone ʋ naahaalʋ yaa ʋ havʋʋbiŋ a tɩaŋ mɩŋ ka mʋ ʋ yi n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ.
translated: The foolish person is the one who hears advice but does not put it into practice; the wise person is the one who hears advice and puts it into practice.

ref: And anyone who gives one of my most humble followers a cup of cool water, just because that person is my follower, will be rewarded.
text: Nɩ sɩmma a baarɩ vuodieke mana dɩ a keŋ a yuori nyasʋnsʋŋ a yɩ n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba gie wʋnyɩ dɩ ʋ yine n kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrʋ wɩa nan daansɩ ye tune.”
translated: If you want to learn something well, you have to listen to those who know more than you and follow their advice.

ref: As John's followers were going away, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What sort of person did you go out into the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
text: Die Jɔɔn kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ yiŋŋine a gara wa, Yisa die dɩ piili a bala yɩa kpɩkpaakʋ Jɔɔn wɩa, “Die nɩ nyɩnna a ga Jɔɔn jigiŋ haagɩrɩ ma wa, bɩa die nɩ tɩŋ a yaala nɩ ye? Die nɩ tɩŋ yile a baarɩ nɩ ye hʋʋtɩ bʋlɔgɩsɩŋ dɩ nɩgɩnana?
translated: When John’s messengers had gone, Jesus began to speak to the crowd about John: “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed swayed by the wind?

ref: to ask Jesus, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
text: dɩ ba ga pɩasɩ wa, “Fʋnɩŋ fʋ yine vuodieke dɩ balla ʋ keŋ wo yaa tɩ chɩsɩma vuogaaŋ bɩbra?”
translated: "When he arrived, he asked, 'Why did you bring this child here to the chief’s house?'"

ref: In the towns where Jesus had worked most of his miracles, the people refused to turn to God. So Jesus was upset with them and said:
text: Vuodiekemba die dɩ benne tɩgɩ diekemba ma ma Yisa die dɩ yine ʋ mamachi wɩa die, ta ka chɩgɩ a nyɩŋ ba tʋmbɩatɩ ma, die wɩa die ʋ galɩŋ ba.
translated: The elders say that if a person does not listen to what his father or mother says, he will not know the right way, and his life will not be good.

ref: So I tell you on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than you. Come to Me and Rest (Luke
text: Ama n bala nɩ, sarɩyaka diile daraaŋ nɩ tɩbɩdatɩŋ nan faasɩ dala a tɩaŋ Sodomi vuosi tɩbɩdatɩŋ.” Nɩ keŋ n jigiŋ a voosi (Luki
translated: Just as it was in the days of Lot, people were eating and drinking, buying and selling, planting and building. But the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all. It will be just like this on the day the Son of Man is revealed.

ref: Take the yoke I give you. Put it on your shoulders and learn from me. I am gentle and humble, and you will find rest.
text: Nɩ nagɩ n chiiti a chii ta nɩ bʋgɩrɩ a nyɩŋ n ma dama n yiwo vuodieke dɩ sʋʋna n gbaŋ tɩŋgbaŋ ta mɩŋŋɩ pɔgɩlɩ n gbaŋ ta nɩ nan ye voosiŋ.
translated: If I had known that you would come, I would have prepared food and called the children to come and greet you.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as Isaiah the prophet had said,
text: Die ʋ yiwo naa amʋ Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Azaya die dɩ bala die wo keŋ yi wusie.
translated: He said that Azaya’s father told him to go to the farm.

ref: “Here is my chosen servant! I love him, and he pleases me. I will give him my Spirit, and he will bring justice to the nations.

text: Bʋa wa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ wa, ‘N kaaŋ ga’ ama ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ʋ tarɩgɩ ʋ sʋŋanyile ta ga kʋakʋ.
translated: The child said to his mother, ‘I want to go but my father said I should stay at home and not go.’

ref: Which one of the sons obeyed his father? “The older one,” the chief priests and leaders answered. Then Jesus told them: You can be sure tax collectors and prostitutes will get into the kingdom of God before you ever will!
text: Yisa die dɩ taanna nandagɩrɩ gie a kpatɩ die ʋ pɩasɩ Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ Juu vuosisi nyɩŋkʋraha dɩ, “Ballɩ bale wo gie, ba jabɩa yine ʋ chɔɔŋ dɩ yaalala dene?” Die ba yiŋŋi a baarɩ, “Jakʋʋrɩ.” Yisa die dɩ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ, “Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, lampotuosiribe aŋaŋ vuodiekemba dɩ dʋaga hɔgʋkpana yaa daakpana nan woliŋ a juu Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma nɩ ma.
translated: Jesus was in the temple courts teaching the people when the Jewish leaders came to him and asked, “By what authority are you doing these things?” He replied, “I will also ask you a question. If you answer me, I will tell you by what authority I am doing these things.”

ref: Jesus asked, “When the owner of that vineyard comes, what do you suppose he will do to those renters?”
text: Yisa die dɩ taanna nandagɩrɩ gie a kpatɩ die ʋ pɩasɩ ba, “Die wɩa kʋakʋ tieŋ dɩ keŋ keŋ bɩa ʋ baa ʋ yi tʋntʋntɩba mi?”
translated: The woman said to her friend at the market, “Why do you always come here every day and yet you never buy anything?”

ref: Once again Jesus used stories to teach the people:
text: Yisa die dɩ bɩ nagɩ nandaga a balɩ wɩa a yɩ vuosisi dɩ,
translated: Take this and give it to your father so that he will buy you clothes.

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a king gave a wedding banquet for his son.
text: “Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ sɩɩ sɩba naaŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ wʋnsɩna hɔgʋhaalʋ banwʋla dɩ ʋ yɩ ʋ bʋa.
translated: My mother said that we should not go outside because it is raining and it is cold.

ref: Others grabbed the servants, then beat them up and killed them.
text: ta bataŋ dɩaŋ die dɩ yigi ʋ yɔŋɩsɩsɩ a nɩgɩ nɩgɩ ba ta kʋʋ ba.
translated: If you do good to people, people will also do good to you.

ref: When the king went in to meet the guests, he found that one of them wasn't wearing the right kind of clothes for the wedding.
text: “Ta Naaŋ wa die dɩ keŋ dɩ ʋ daansɩ ye vuodiekemba dɩ kenne banwʋlaka, die ʋ ye daa wʋnyɩ dɩ benne vuosi sʋnsʋŋ ta ka yeegi banwʋla nyiŋyeeke.
translated: If someone wants to build a house, they must first gather the materials, then they can start the work and finish the house well.

ref: At last the woman died.
text: Ka kʋaŋ kʋaŋ chaaŋ hɔgʋ wa dɩaŋ die dɩ kpi.
translated: The old man went to the farm early in the morning.

ref: The second most important commandment is like this one. And it is, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: Ta jadieke dɩaŋ dɩ diisine bule wo dɩaŋ bɩ yiwo nɩŋŋmɩna a gʋtɩ, ‘Chome fʋ chanchaaŋ sɩba fʋ gbaŋ gbaŋ.’
translated: He told the child to go and call his mother and say to her, ‘Mother, you should come quickly.’

ref: No one was able to give Jesus an answer, and from that day on, no one dared ask him any more questions.
text: Die ba wʋnyɩ mana die ka bɩagɩ a yiŋŋi a balɩ wɩɩŋ, die wɩa a nyɩŋ dɩnda daarɩ mi yaa gamma vuoŋ die ka bɩ yaa sikimiŋ a baaŋ nan bɩagɩ a pɩasɩ Yisa wʋpɩasɩka.
translated: If you believe in him and accept him into your heart, he will give you eternal life and you will become a child of God through faith in Jesus Christ.

ref: And now your temple will be deserted.
text: Ye, Ŋmɩŋ va nɩ Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ ta ka zie yɔrɩ.
translated: Yes, God gave me God’s wisdom so that I will not be afraid.

ref: So obey everything they teach you, but don't do as they do. After all, they say one thing and do something else.
text: Die chɩaŋ ma dɩ vɩɩna aŋ nɩ saagɩ ta dɩa ba dagɩna nɩ die mana, ama nɩ sɩmma nɩ keŋ yime wudieke ba yinene, dama ba ka dɩ wudieke ba balala wa.
translated: If you do not have money and you want to buy something, you can ask your friend, but if your friend does not have what you want, then you will not get what you want.

ref: They pile heavy burdens on people's shoulders and won't lift a finger to help.
text: Ba ŋaaŋ vaa wa vuosi chii chiidʋnsa, chii dieke dɩ dʋnsɩna a yɩa ba, ama ba ka saaga a suŋŋe be aŋ ba chii chiititi.
translated: If someone wants to do something good, they should do it with all their heart, but if they do it just to show off, it is not good.

ref: None of you should be called the leader. The Messiah is your only leader.
text: Nɩ daa bɩ vaa vuosi wa nɩ ba ‘Nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩba’ dama nɩ yaa wa nɩ Nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩ balɩmɩŋ wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine Yisa Masia.
translated: We have come here today to talk about ‘Nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩrɩ’ because Nɩŋŋandɩɩsɩ is the word of Jesus Messiah.

ref: And anyone who swears by the temple also swears by God, who lives there.
text: Ta dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ huu Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ juokpeŋkpɩɩkʋ, dɩ dagɩya sɩba ʋ huu kanɩŋ aŋaŋ Ŋmɩŋ, vuodieke dɩ benne ka ma.
translated: They all went to the river to fetch water, but when they arrived there, they saw that the river was dry, so they returned home.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. Yet you neglect the more important matters of the Law, such as justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
text: “Nɩnɩŋ mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba aŋaŋ Farasisi mba die yine gɩgaantɩŋ, wʋbɩaŋ bie nɩ ma. Nɩ naga nanjʋʋsɩ aŋaŋ bʋŋʋ aŋaŋ neeri aŋaŋ nyinti a taŋ baŋ sʋŋ a nagɩ kaanɩ a yɩa Ŋmɩŋ, ama ta va jadieke dɩ yine nɩŋŋmɩna ʋ mɩraha ma; sɩba wusie sarɩya diile, aŋaŋ zɔɔlɩŋ chɩgɩla aŋaŋ yada yiile. Genhe die mʋna nɩ yime, ta da va hanɩŋ ba yiile.
translated: When you learn to speak French very well, you will be able to go far. If you want to travel to other places and meet people and see new things that you have not seen before, you need to know it, but if you do not learn it well, you will not benefit from it; you will just be sitting in one place, watching others do things. That is why I am telling you, so that you will try to learn it.

ref: That's what you are like. Outside you look good, but inside you are evil and only pretend to be good.
text: Die gbaŋ gbaŋ ta vuosi yese nɩ yeŋ me ta nɩ vɩɩna, ama nɩ sʋgɩtɩ dɩ suuli aŋaŋ ŋmɩnchɩbɩsɩ aŋaŋ bɩaŋ. Yisa die dɩ bala ba tɩbɩdatɩŋ wɩa (Luki
translated: They all put their hands together to work, and they did not quarrel or fight with each other. That is how they finished building the house quickly (Luke

ref: You will soon hear about wars and threats of wars, but don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
text: Nɩ nan wʋŋ tɔntɩ watɩ nɩ chaaŋ aŋaŋ saasaa wɩa, ama nɩ da vaa ŋmaamɩŋ yalla nɩ, dama sie wɩaha gie yi, ama a ka dagɩ sɩba dʋnɩa kpatɩkʋ keŋye mɩŋ.
translated: If you want to do something good for yourself, but you do not have enough strength, ask your friends for help, but do not forget to thank them after the work is done.

ref: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
text: “Saŋka mi dɩɩ yi vuoŋ dɩ a keŋ a balɩ nɩ dɩ, ‘Nɩ ye, Masia wa wʋnna yaa ʋ benne mi wo’, nɩ daa keŋ yi wo yada;
translated: So I went and told him what you said, ‘Yes, the Messiah has come and is staying with me,’ and then he believed what I said;

ref: And pray that you won't have to escape in winter or on a Sabbath.
text: Nɩ jʋʋsɩma Ŋmɩŋ aŋ ʋ da keŋ yi wahalaha keŋ yɩbɩŋ saŋŋa yaa davʋʋsɩkɩrɩ daraaŋ.
translated: If you want something, you have to work hard and be patient to achieve your goals.

ref: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
text: “Ama vuoŋ ka sɩba daarɩ yaa saŋŋa dieke n baaŋ keŋ; malakasi dieke dɩ benne arɩzanna ma dɩaŋ ka sɩba, manɩŋ Ŋmɩŋ Bʋa gbaŋ ka sɩba, sie n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ nyɩɩna sɩbɩna.
translated: If you want to do good, do it for everyone; do not do good only for those who are close to you, but do good for everyone, because God wants us to do good to all people.

ref: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
text: Die wɩa nɩ gbara dama nɩ zɩ daa dieke manɩŋ nɩ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ bala n keŋ.
translated: They went to the farm and found many yams there because it had rained a lot.

ref: But suppose one of the servants thinks the master won't return until late.
text: Ama ʋ yi yɔmʋbɩaŋ, ʋ nan dɩ yile ʋ sʋŋ ma dɩ ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ wo kaaŋ keŋ lagɩ lagɩ,
translated: If you do good, you will see that your goodness will follow you everywhere you go.

ref: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
text: “Yɔmʋ bɩa yine yɩantieŋ ta yie ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ dagɩna dɩ ʋ yi wudieke? Wʋnɩŋ ʋ yine vuodieke ʋ Yɔmʋtieŋ dɩ nan vʋarɩ dɩ ʋ daansɩma ʋ yɔmʋ chanchaalɩba ta yɩma ba ba nyindiike saŋŋa dieke dɩ mʋna.
translated: God made the world and everything in it. Do you know that everything God made has a purpose and He wants us to use them well and be thankful for what we have?

ref: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Wusie maŋ bala nɩ, ʋ nan nagɩ wa a zieŋ dɩ ʋ daansɩma ʋ nyinti mana mana.
translated: If you do that, he will see that you are truly his good friend.

ref: The ones who were wise took along extra oil for their lamps.
text: ama ta sɩsɩbɩrɩba die dɩ nagɩ kpaaŋ a siti siti a yi kɔlɩbɩsɩ ma a gʋtɩ pɔgɩlɩ aŋaŋ popolisi.
translated: The woman was walking when she saw a child crying because the police had arrested his mother.

ref: When the women got up and started getting their lamps ready,
text: Saŋka mi hɔgʋlɩaba baŋka mana die dɩ hagɩ a ŋmɩɩlɩ ba popoli dɩ jʋalɩ.
translated: If you want to be successful, you must work hard and be patient.

ref: The kingdom is also like what happened when a man went away and put his three servants in charge of all he owned.
text: “Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ wɩa nan bɩ sɩɩ sɩba tigiŋ tieŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ bala ʋ dɩa, ta wa ʋ yɔŋɩsɩ a tɩa ba ligire dɩ ba tuo a dime nyʋʋŋ.
translated: When the chief dies, they take his body and lay it in the room, and then they call the elders and the young men to come and wash the corpse.

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Dama vuodieke mana dɩ yalla nyinti, ba nan ye nyinti pam a gʋtɩ amʋ ba faasɩ yallɩma pam. Ta vuodieke dɩaŋ dɩ wone jaaŋ ba nan tuo bɩta gbaŋ ʋ yalla wa a nyɩŋ ʋ jigiŋ.
translated: If someone does something for you, you should also do the same for others so that the help will go round. But if you only receive and do not give, then the help will not reach everyone.

ref: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
text: “Ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ n nan yiŋŋi a balɩ a yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ benne n nuugalɩ ka chaakʋ, ‘Nɩnɩŋ vuobɩatɩ gie, nɩ vɩɩna aŋaŋ tɩbɩdatɩŋ, nɩ chʋnna ga ta va mɩŋ. Nɩ gamma a ga juu boli dieke dɩ kana a ka kpise ta Ŋmɩŋ dɩ wʋnsɩna a gbarɩ Sitaani aŋaŋ ʋ vuosi wo.
translated: Then the chief said to them, ‘If you do this, you will bring trouble upon yourselves, and you will not have peace. You must always remember that God is the one who created us and not Satan who wants to destroy us.’

ref: “You know two days from now will be Passover. This is when the Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies and nailed to a cross.”
text: “Nɩ sɩba a baarɩ dɩ vʋntʋŋ yine gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋga, ta ba nan nagɩ manɩŋ vuota Bʋa a yi vuosi nuusi me dɩ ba kpaasɩ mɩŋ a dɩɩsɩ dagarɩkʋ ma.”
translated: If you go to the farm and you see a snake lying on the path, do not kill it because Bua said that if you kill snakes, you will have problems in your life.

ref: We could have sold this perfume for a lot of money and given it to the poor.”
text: Tulaaribu gie tɩŋ nan bɩagɩ daa ligirikpɩɩma a nagɩ ligirehe a suŋŋi zɔɔlɩntieliŋ!”
translated: Let us go and see the place where they say the elders sit and settle disputes!

ref: Then they sang a hymn and went out to the Mount of Olives. Peter's Promise (Mark
text: Die ba wa chɩa Ŋmɩŋ jɩamɩŋ yɩlɩ, ka kʋaŋ chaaŋ ba chʋŋ ga Olivi Tɩɩsɩ kunkogiri me. Yisa die dɩ bala dɩ Piita nan chiisi dɩ ʋ ka sɩba wa dene (Maki
translated: They went to Ŋmɩŋ’s house yesterday, and after that they met with Olivi Tɩɩsɩ at the market. When they arrived, Piita was not there and his mother said he had gone to Maki.

ref: and from then on he started looking for a good chance to betray Jesus. Jesus Eats the Passover Meal with His Disciples (Mark
text: Die a nyɩŋ daarɩ mi Judasi die wa a paalɩ a yaala sievɩɩnɩŋ dɩ ʋ nagɩ Yisa a yi ba nuusi me. Yisa aŋaŋ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ dine gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋga nyindiike dene (Maki
translated: One of those days, Judas went to the leaders to betray Jesus to them. Jesus knew what Judas was planning to do and told his disciples about it (Mark)

ref: On the first day of the Festival of Thin Bread, Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Where do you want us to prepare the Passover meal?”
text: Ta paanʋ dieke die ba kana a ka yi dabɔtɩ dʋʋga piiliku daarɩ, ta Yisa kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba die dɩ keŋ ʋ jigiŋ a pɩasɩ wa, “Sɩa fʋ yaala tɩ ga wʋnsɩ a yɩ fʋ a gbara gasɩtɩaŋkʋ dʋʋga nyindiike daraaŋ?”
translated: And as they were walking along the road, someone said to Jesus, “I will follow you wherever you go, but what will I get if I leave everything behind?”

ref: From now on I am not going to drink any wine, until I drink new wine with you in my Father's kingdom.”
text: N bala nɩ, n kaaŋ bɩ nyu daabʋ gie, sie daa dieke manɩŋ aŋaŋ nɩ dɩ bala tɩ lagɩsɩ a nyu daahaalɩbʋ n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ ma, ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ.”
translated: And so, I said that if you do not listen, then you will not understand what I am saying because this matter is very important, my friend.

ref: He came back and found his disciples sleeping. So he said to Peter, “Can't any of you stay awake with me for just one hour?
text: Die ʋ yiŋŋi ga ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba bataa wa jigiŋ ta ye ta ba gʋʋra; die ʋ pɩasɩ Piita, “Die wɩa nɩ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yesime aŋaŋ mɩŋ saŋŋa bɩta gbaŋ?
translated: He was sitting with his disciples when a woman came to him with a gift; he asked Peter, "Why do you think this woman has done such a thing for me?"

ref: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a large mob armed with swords and clubs was with him. They had been sent by the chief priests and the nation's leaders.
text: Ʋ nʋaŋ die ye ka nan tɩŋgbaŋ, ta Judasi, ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba baŋ aŋaŋ bale wo wʋnyɩ dɩ keŋ tʋgɩ mi, aŋaŋ kpɩkpaaŋ, bataŋ dɩ pɔgɩlɩ jɩbɩwagɩtɩ ta bataŋ dɩaŋ dɩ pɔgɩlɩ dengbile, die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ Juu vuosi nyɩŋkʋraha tʋnna ba.
translated: The elders and leaders met together, and Judas, who betrayed him, was with them. They discussed how they might arrest Jesus by trickery and kill him, but they said, “Not during the festival, or the people may riot.”

ref: One of Jesus' followers pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
text: Die ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba wʋnyɩ dɩ vʋarɩ jɩbɩwaŋ a chɩa gobi Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba jakʋʋŋ yɔmʋ tɩbɩŋ a taaŋ.
translated: When the elders gather, they tell stories about how people used to live in the olden days.

ref: Again Peter denied it, and this time he swore, “I don't even know that man!”
text: Die Piita die dɩ bɩ chiisike aŋaŋ huusi dɩ, “N ka sɩba daa wa!”
translated: The father called his child and said, "Come here quickly!"

ref: But in front of everyone Peter said, “That isn't so! I don't know what you are talking about!”
text: Ama ta Piita die dɩ baarɩ ʋ ka sɩba wa ba mana nɩŋŋa. Dɩ, “N ka sɩba vuodieke nɩ balala wa wɩa.”
translated: But Peter said to them, "I do not know what you are talking about."

ref: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
text: Die dɩ bɩna a yi bɩta, vuodiekemba dɩ zene mi wo dɩ keŋ Piita jigiŋ a balɩ wa dɩ, “Wusie, yiwo ba wʋnyɩ dama fʋ bala sɩba Galili vuoŋ!”
translated: While he was still speaking, suddenly a crowd came up, and the man called Judas, one of the Twelve, was leading them. He approached Jesus to kiss him, but Jesus asked him, “Judas, are you betraying the Son of Man with a kiss?”

ref: and Peter remembered that Jesus had said, “Before a rooster crows, you will say three times you don't know me.” Then Peter went out and cried bitterly.
text: die Piita dɩ tɩɩnsɩ wudieke Yisa dɩ bala a yɩ wa wa dɩ, “Kparaaŋ nan keŋ baa ka kʋmma, nan chiisi mɩŋ bʋtaa dɩ ka sɩba mɩŋ.” Ta Piita die dɩ nyɩŋ a kʋŋ pam.
translated: Then Peter answered and said to Jesus, "Lord, if it is you, tell me to come to you on the water." So Peter got out of the boat.

ref: Pilate knew the leaders had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.
text: Dama Paliti die sɩba a baarɩ haasɩ wɩa die ba yigi Yisa a keŋ yɩ wa.
translated: The woman Paliti took her child and went to the farm where she met Jesus on the way.

ref: Jesus was brought before Pilate the governor, who asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” “Those are your words!” Jesus answered.
text: Yisa die zie wo tɩŋgbaŋka jakʋʋrɩ Paliti nɩŋŋa; ta Paliti die pɩasɩ wa dɩ, “Fʋnɩŋ, fʋ yine Juu vuosi naaŋ wa?” Yisa die dɩ yiŋŋi a balɩ, “Wa, fʋnɩŋ fʋ balala.”
translated: They brought him to the governor Pilate; and Pilate asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” He answered him, “Yes, you say so.”

ref: And when the chief priests and leaders brought their charges against him, he did not say a thing.
text: Ama die Ŋmɩŋ kɩkaabɩtɩba nyɩŋkʋraha aŋaŋ tɩka nyɩŋkʋraha nɩŋ die dɩ galɩnnana wa, die ʋ ka balɩ ba wɩɩŋ.
translated: If you do not respect elders and you do not listen to what elders say, you will not learn anything.

ref: They made a crown out of thorn branches and placed it on his head, and they put a stick in his right hand. The soldiers knelt down and pretended to worship him. They made fun of him and shouted, “Hey, you king of the Jews!”
text: ta die a nagɩ haŋgɔɔsɩ a wʋnsɩ sibubiŋ a nagɩ ka a bubi wo ta a nagɩ daaŋgbanɩŋ a yɩ ʋ nuudiigiŋ me, ta die a wa gbirigi ʋ nɩŋŋa a vʋarɩ wa fala, die a baarɩ dɩ, “Juu vuosi naaŋ wa, tɩ waasa fʋ!”
translated: He took the child and placed him on his lap and the child started to cry, so he took a coin and gave it to him, but the child refused it, then he said, "If you don't want this, then go away!"

ref: The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.
text: Die ba kpaasɩna wa dagarɩkʋ ma a kpatɩ wa, die ba nagɩ ʋ nyiŋyeeke a puo taŋ aŋaŋ chaachaa,
translated: If someone is hungry and you give them food, but you do not speak kindly to them, it is like you have not helped them at all.

ref: Then they sat down to guard him.
text: die ba sʋʋŋ kalɩ a gbarɩ wa.
translated: they have already built a house here.

ref: Others said, “Wait! Let's see if Elijah will come and save him.”
text: Ama ʋ chanchaalɩba dɩ baarɩ dɩ, “Vaa wa aŋ tɩ ye sɩba Elaja nan keŋ a gbatɩ wa a taaŋ?”
translated: But the elders said, “Who among us can go with Elijah so that he will not deceive us?”
